title: Snippet manager with version history tracking
purpose: Manages code snippets with CRUD operations and maintains version history for each snippet. Used to store, retrieve, and version control code snippets in a session.
module_context: Exports a SnippetManager class that handles snippet storage, indexing, and version history tracking with asynchronous file I/O operations.

steps.content: Create the main snippets directory if it does not exist.
Create the history directory if it does not exist.
Scan the snippets directory for existing snippet files.
For each snippet file, attempt to read its metadata and add it to the in-memory index.
Log errors encountered while indexing individual snippets but continue processing.
Iterate through all indexed snippets.
For each snippet, attempt to read its content from disk.
IF reading fails, create a snippet entry with empty content.
Sort the snippets by last modified timestamp in descending order.
Return the sorted list of snippets.
Look up the snippet metadata in the index using the provided ID.
IF the snippet is not found in the index, return null.
Attempt to read the snippet content from disk.
IF reading fails, log the error and return a snippet with empty content.
Return the snippet with its content and metadata.
Look up the snippet metadata in the index using the provided ID.
IF the snippet is not found, throw an error indicating the snippet does not exist.
Validate that the content size does not exceed the maximum allowed file size.
Write the updated content to the snippet file on disk.
Update the snippet's metadata with the new modification timestamp.
Record the updated content in the snippet's version history.
IF recording history fails, log a warning but continue with the save operation.
Validate that the snippet name is a non-empty string.
Validate that the snippet content is provided and not null or undefined.
Validate that the content size does not exceed the maximum allowed file size.
Sanitize the snippet name to generate a valid ID.
IF the sanitized name is empty, throw an error indicating the name must contain alphanumeric characters.
Check if a snippet with the generated ID already exists.
IF a duplicate exists, throw an error indicating the snippet already exists.
Write the initial content to a new snippet file on disk.
Update the snippet metadata with the file path and modification timestamp.
Register the new snippet in the in-memory index.
Record the initial version in the snippet's history file.
IF recording history fails, log a warning but continue with the creation.
Return the generated snippet ID.
Look up the snippet metadata in the index using the provided ID.
IF the snippet is not found, throw an error indicating the snippet does not exist.
Delete the snippet file from disk.
IF deletion fails, log the error and throw an error indicating the deletion failed.
Delete the snippet's history file from disk.
IF history deletion fails, log a warning but continue with the deletion.
Remove the snippet from the in-memory index.
Construct the path to the snippet's history file.
Attempt to load existing version history from the history file.
IF loading fails, start with an empty history array.
Create a new version entry with the current timestamp and content.
Add the new version entry to the history array.
Trim the history array to keep only the last 100 versions.
Write the updated history back to the history file.
Look up the snippet metadata in the index using the provided ID.
IF the snippet is not found, throw an error indicating the snippet does not exist.
Construct the path to the snippet's history file.
IF the history file does not exist, return an empty array.
Read the history file content from disk.
Parse the history content as JSON and return the version entries.
IF parsing fails, log the error and throw an error indicating retrieval failed.
Retrieve the version history for the specified snippet.
Iterate through the history entries in chronological order.
Find the most recent version entry that occurred at or before the specified timestamp.
IF no such version exists, return null.
ELSE return the content of the found version.
Attempt to get the file statistics for the provided path.
IF statistics retrieval succeeds, update the index with the new metadata.
IF statistics retrieval fails, log the error and do nothing.
Remove the snippet entry from the in-memory index using the provided ID.
Clear all entries from the in-memory index.
Return the number of entries currently in the in-memory index.
Check if a snippet with the provided ID exists in the in-memory index.
Return true if the snippet exists, false otherwise.
